summary: LIZAM Phase 1 backend tested end-to-end against external URL. All 16 pytest cases pass: health, public site-settings/home-content/publications/authors/categories seeding, auth (login/login-wrong/me/me-unauth/register/register-duplicate/logout/refresh), admin overview (401/403/200), and MongoDB _id leak guard across every response.

minor.issue: Rotating access_token via Set-Cookie works and /me still succeeds after, but if called within the same second the JWT payload is identical to the previous one (same iat/exp because create_access_token uses 60-minute expiry with minute-level rotation). Not a failure — just note that a quick double-refresh returns functionally equivalent tokens.
No password strength validation beyond min_length=6 (Pydantic). Acceptable for Phase 1 scaffolding but worth tightening later.
Response has `items/total/limit/offset` but individual items do NOT expose `content_html_ar/en` (intentionally projected out). Confirmed expected for list view; detail endpoint not yet in Phase 1.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses FastAPI deprecated @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown'); migrate to lifespan context manager before production.
Admin role guard treats `editor` and `reviewer` as admin — fine per spec, but `/admin/overview` currently exposes full counts to all four roles; consider finer permission checks in later phases.
bcrypt.gensalt() default cost=12 is fine; bcrypt hashes verified to start with $2b$ via login flow.
CORS uses allow_origin_regex=r'https?://.*' with allow_credentials=True — this is very permissive; restrict to explicit frontend origins for production.
seed_on_startup is idempotent via $setOnInsert and updates admin password if changed — matches playbook.
Publications seed relies on cats/authors being seeded first in same function call; ordering within seed_on_startup is correct. Safe.
server.py is 648 lines in a single file — acceptable for Phase 1 but will exceed the 700-line guideline quickly; plan module split (auth/, public/, admin/, models/, seed/).
No rate-limiting / brute-force lockout on /auth/login (playbook mentions 5-fail lockout). Not implemented — flag for Phase 2.
test_credentials: Super Admin admin@lizam.sa / Lizam@2026 — verified working.
seed_data_creation: Test creates unique TEST_user_<uuid>@example.com registered accounts per run; no cleanup (acceptable — isolated by uuid). Admin seed + site/home/categories/authors/publications all verified present.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend external URL: https://lizam-legal.preview.emergentagent.com. Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_lizam_backend.py -v` for regression. Auth uses httpOnly cookies (samesite=none, secure=true) — use requests.Session() to persist. Admin roles accepted: super_admin, admin, editor, reviewer. Brute-force lockout NOT yet implemented. Phase 2 will add admin CRUD; reuse this test file and extend.
rca of the issue: N/A — all tests passing. One test iteration needed a fix (email-lowercasing assertion) which was in the test, not backend code.
